hypothesis: If a computational framework combining fragment-based polymer generation, DFT-refined force field parameters, and Bayesian optimization with pre-trained molecular representations is applied to the design space of hypothetical polyamines, then it will efficiently identify high-performance CO2 adsorbents with superior uptake capacity compared to known reference polyamines within a limited simulation budget, because the surrogate models can learn structure-property relationships from sparse labeled data and guide exploration toward optimal regions of chemical space.
